summary: A three-tiered stepped pagoda-style tower with a central courtyard. The structure features alternating layers of wood and stonebrick, with thatch/hay bale accents on the exterior walls. Each tier steps inward progressively, creating a pyramid-like silhouette. A small decorative tower or finial crowns the top. The building is surrounded by a stonebrick foundation wall and sits on a prepared dirt platform.

shape.overall_form: stepped pyramid with three concentric rectangular tiers decreasing in size upward
shape.symmetry: four-fold rotational symmetry around central axis

materials.name: wood
materials.role: primary wall cladding on each tier

materials.name: hay_bale
materials.role: decorative exterior accents and wall sections

materials.name: glass
materials.role: windows on middle and lower tiers

materials.name: fence
materials.role: railings and decorative elements on tiers

elements: internal structure with dark interior space
rebuild_hints: Build three concentric rectangular platforms, each smaller than the one below
Layer 1 (bottom): approximately 18x18 blocks, walls of wood with stonebrick reinforcement
Layer 2 (middle): approximately 14x14 blocks, set back 2 blocks from layer 1 edges
Layer 3 (top): approximately 10x10 blocks, set back 2 blocks from layer 2 edges
Add hay bale decorative sections on exterior walls of each tier
Place windows (glass) on middle and lower tier walls, spaced regularly
Create central courtyard/hollow interior space visible from top
Build perimeter stonebrick wall foundation around entire structure
Add small decorative tower/finial structure on top tier roof
Use wooden planks for primary wall material with stonebrick trim/reinforcement
Ensure each tier has a flat roof before building the next tier up
uncertainties: Exact interior layout and room divisions are not clearly visible
Precise window placement and size cannot be determined from angles shown
Whether the central courtyard is open to sky or has a roof is ambiguous
The exact composition of the decorative finial on top is unclear
Interior floor materials and layout unknown
Exact block count for each tier difficult to determine precisely from perspective
